# Codex Styler 0.2.0-beta.2 — Companion Studio Refinement

This Beta turns the first Companion Studio implementation into a more coherent, resilient creative workspace and adds localized update highlights before download.

Release tag: `v0.2.0-beta.2`

## Download and install

### macOS 13+ on Apple Silicon

1. Download `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.2_aarch64-unsigned.dmg`.
2. Compare its SHA-256 digest with `SHA256SUMS.txt`.
3. Open the DMG and drag Codex Styler to Applications.
4. On first launch, Control-click Codex Styler and choose **Open**.

The macOS app is ad-hoc signed, does not have an Apple Developer ID signature, and is not notarized. Do not disable Gatekeeper globally.

### Windows 11 x64 — community-validation Beta

1. Download `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.2_x64-unsigned-setup.exe`.
2. Compare its SHA-256 digest with `SHA256SUMS.txt`.
3. Review the GitHub build attestation, then run the per-user installer.

The Windows installer is not Authenticode-signed, so SmartScreen may show an unknown-publisher warning. It is built on GitHub-hosted Windows runners and structurally verified, but Windows remains explicitly community-validated until independent real-device diagnostics are attached.

## Companion Studio refinements

- Rebuilt Import, Extract, Clean up, Align, Calibrate, Motions, and Test & Save as one consistent professional workspace without adding a second navigation hierarchy.
- Added real picker and native file drag-and-drop ingestion with validation for static images, naturally sorted sequences, video, and sprite atlases.
- Broadened system-WebView video handling, added reliable frame extraction fallbacks, and made codec failures distinguish the container from its actual encoded stream.
- Added non-destructive cleanup controls, source/result comparison, sampled-color removal, edge inspection backgrounds, correction strokes, corner masks, discard, apply, and reset actions.
- Added shared-canvas alignment diagnostics, common crop and scale suggestions, ground-line checks, onion-skin comparison, and per-frame baseline correction.
- Added visual direction calibration with anchor coverage, seam and reverse-motion diagnostics, exact angle binding, frame navigation, and timeline feedback.
- Added pose-aware idle-motion range authoring, playback preview, overlap checks, saved-motion management, and reduced-motion behavior.
- Added an interactive final stage for pointer direction, dragging, snapping, composer height, size, position, smoothing, and render-cap testing.
- Kept undo, redo, autosave, step reset, project reset, and destructive-action confirmation available without forcing every tool into a linear mini-wizard.

## Import, packaging, and library fixes

- Built-in companion cards now use dedicated alpha-clean portrait assets rather than slicing arbitrary cells from a runtime atlas.
- Source validation reports rejected files before work begins and preserves supported native drop paths.
- Project persistence caches derived frames outside lightweight settings and restores normalized projects safely.
- Package validation now checks companion metadata, frame and atlas bounds, declared assets, and license fields more consistently.
- Shared frame cropping and compilation preserve character scale and ground position across an animation.

## Localized update information

- The update check still discovers versions and signed installers through GitHub Releases.
- Each Release now includes `release-notes.json` with structured English and Simplified Chinese summaries.
- Codex Styler fetches this small metadata file before download, selects the current application language, and falls back to English and then the updater manifest text.
- Update content is rendered as safe structured text; no remote HTML, script, image, or theme code is executed.

## Automated evidence

- Desktop component tests cover update localization, creator state, import, calibration, motion ranges, persistence, live companion selection, and restart behavior.
- Theme-core tests cover companion archives, declared assets, licenses, frame/page bounds, and migration.
- Rust tests cover update channels, localized release-note selection and version matching, Codex discovery, loopback targets, diagnostics, and archive safety.
- Browser E2E includes English dark/light creator views, compact layouts, video extraction, reset confirmation, companion library portraits, and settings.

## Release evidence

- `SHA256SUMS.txt` covers both installers and the macOS updater archive.
- `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.2.spdx.json` exports the dependency graph as SPDX 2.3.
- GitHub artifact attestations cover both installers; platform updater artifacts carry Tauri signatures.
- `latest.json` contains the macOS Apple Silicon and Windows x64 signed update entries.
- `release-notes.json` contains validated localized update summaries for the pre-download panel.

Verify installers with GitHub CLI:

```bash
gh attestation verify Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.2_aarch64-unsigned.dmg \
  --repo xuhuanstudio/codex-styler

gh attestation verify Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.2_x64-unsigned-setup.exe \
  --repo xuhuanstudio/codex-styler
```

## Beta limitations

- Windows 11 real-device install, managed restart, sleep/wake, update, restore, and uninstall evidence is still required before the release-candidate gate.
- macOS Intel, Windows ARM64, Linux, multiple entities, video backgrounds, 3D/WebGL, and arbitrary AI background segmentation remain outside 0.2.
- Video decode support depends on system media codecs. Unsupported streams receive conversion guidance instead of silently continuing.
- Complex backgrounds may need manual correction strokes or external preparation; local cleanup does not claim universal automatic segmentation.
- macOS notarization and Windows Authenticode signing remain required before v1 Stable.

Codex Styler is unofficial and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by OpenAI.
